behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This tests request construction logic—whether the taxonomy list is correctly serialized into the outgoing payload. The failure (truncated or filtered labels) is purely local to the request-building function. The API call can be mocked; no external infrastructure is needed. A unit test mocking the HTTP call and inspecting the request body directly will catch missing or filtered labels.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API call and verify that the request payload includes all taxonomy labels from the configured taxonomy. Inspect the request body to ensure no labels are truncated, filtered, or dropped. Catches logic errors in request construction.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests response validation logic—a pure function that checks whether an API response value is in a whitelist and substitutes a default if not. The logic is entirely local; the API response can be mocked. A unit test mocking the API to return an out-of-taxonomy label and verifying the module returns 'unknown' will catch missing or broken validation.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a label not in the taxonomy and verify the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the invalid label to the caller. Catches missing validation, validation logic errors, or accidental forwarding of unexpected values.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: This tests caching logic—whether the module reuses a cached result on the second invocation rather than re-calling the API. A unit test mocking the API and verifying it is called exactly once across two invocations will catch missing or broken caching. The cache itself is local state; no external backend is required for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API, call the classify function twice with the same document, and verify the API is called exactly once with the cached result returned on the second call. Catches missing caching, incorrect cache keys, or failure to reuse cached results.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This tests whether real or realistic documents actually stay within the token budget—a behavioral property that depends on accurate token counting, not just budget-check logic. A unit test with a mocked token counter that always returns safe values would pass even if real documents exceed the budget in production. Real failure requires realistic token counting (matching the API's tokenizer) and representative test documents. An integration test with a real or library-provided tokenizer and typical documents will verify actual token consumption stays within limits.
behaviors.test_description: Use a realistic token counter (library or API-matched tokenizer), process representative documents of typical size, and verify the total tokens consumed stays within the configured per-call budget. Catches inaccurate token counting, missing budget enforcement, or inadequate document truncation/summarization logic.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests retry and backoff logic—a purely local state machine that detects a rate-limit error, delays, and retries. The API response can be mocked to return the rate-limit signal; the delay mechanism (sleep, timer) can be mocked to verify the wait occurs. A unit test mocking the API to fail once, mocking the time/delay function, and verifying the retry succeeds will catch missing retry, missing waits, or errors being propagated instead of retried.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a rate-limit error (429 or equivalent) on the first call and success on the second. Mock the time/delay mechanism and verify the module waits for a configured retry delay before retrying. Verify the final result is returned to the caller, not an error. Catches missing retry logic, missing waits, or unhandled rate-limit errors.
